macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Fed Chair Powell Announces No Rate Change, Cites Middle East Conflict and Inflation Pressures
items.summary: The Federal Reserve maintained the federal funds rate target at 3.5% to 3.75%, with Powell stating he had 'no choice' amid inflation at 3.5% PCE driven by Middle East oil price surges and economic uncertainty. Economic activity remains solid with resilient consumer spending, though housing is weak.

items.title: US National Debt Surpasses 100% of GDP, Hits Record $38.97 Trillion
items.summary: The US national debt held by the public exceeded 100% of GDP for the first time since WWII, reaching $38,967,833,861,543 as of May 1, 2026, amid projections to double in three decades due to spending.

items.title: Q1 GDP Grows 2%, Below Expectations; AI Investment and Consumer Spending Drive Growth
items.summary: US first-quarter GDP expanded at 2%, slightly under the 2.3% forecast, supported by equipment investment in AI/computers and resilient consumer spending.

items.title: Gas Prices Surge to $4.39 National Average as Oil Tensions Rise
items.summary: AAA national average gas price jumped 9 cents in one day to $4.392, linked to Middle East conflict and US naval blockade impacting Iran's economy and global oil supply.

items.title: 30-Year Mortgage Rates Climb to 6.3% Amid Economic Pressures
items.summary: The average 30-year fixed mortgage rate rose to 6.3% from 6.23% the prior week, per Freddie Mac data, reflecting higher borrowing costs.

items.title: Powell Pledges to Remain as Fed Governor Post-Term, Rejects Retirement
items.summary: Fed Chair Jerome Powell stated he will not retire after his term ends next month, committing to serve as a constructive Fed governor amid discussions on his future and Fed independence.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Hit by $560M in Liquidations Amid US-Iran Tensions
items.summary: Over the past 24 hours, more than $560 million worth of cryptocurrency positions have been liquidated, closely tied to escalating US-Iran tensions impacting market volatility.

items.title: Bitcoin Next Breakout Could Shock Everyone as Price Nears Key Levels
items.summary: Bitcoin has risen from $2,000 yesterday to approach $783 with potential to push into the 80s amid low liquidity, signaling a possible rapid breakout; discussions include Trump comments on Iran oil blockade and upcoming Clarity Act announcement.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: GPT-5.5 Achieves Top Position on AI Benchmarks
items.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 has claimed the top spot on independent AI benchmarks within 24 hours of release, scoring 60 points and representing a significant architectural redesign from previous versions.

items.title: DeepSeek V4 Pro Dominates Open-Source AI Race
items.summary: China's DeepSeek V4 Pro, a government-backed model with 1.6 trillion parameters, is rapidly winning the open-source AI competition while US labs increasingly close their models, creating potential strategic dependencies.

items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude for Creative Work Integration
items.summary: Anthropic released official Claude connectors for creative applications including Blender, Adobe Creative Cloud, Ableton, Splice, Canva, SketchUp, and Resolume, with a 240,000 euro corporate donation to Blender.

items.title: Apple's Internal AI Practices Revealed Through Leaked Files
items.summary: Apple accidentally shipped internal CLAUDE.md files in a production app, exposing that engineers use Anthropic's Claude model and an internal LLM called Juno AI for development and customer support.

items.title: Stripe Launches AI Agent Commerce Tools
items.summary: Stripe introduced Stripe Projects, the Machine Payments Protocol, and Link Wallet for Agents, enabling AI agents to autonomously build, buy, and sell products.

items.title: Grok 4.3 Beta and Expanded AI Model Leaderboards
items.summary: New AI model releases including Grok 4.3 Beta are competing on updated leaderboards, with growing context windows and agentic harnesses becoming standard requirements for all major AI models.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Heroes of Might and Magic: Olden Era Launches in Early Access with Massive Success
items.summary: Heroes of Might and Magic: Olden Era has launched into Early Access, selling over 250,000 copies in less than 24 hours and receiving over 90% positive Steam reviews. Co-op mode and teams are confirmed to be coming soon.

items.title: New VR Games Released: Five Nights at Freddy's, TMNT, and More
items.summary: This week's VR releases include Five Nights at Freddy's: Secret Of The Mimic on PSVR 2 and PCVR, One More Delve on Quest and PCVR, Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Empire City on PCVR and Quest, and Microsoft Flight Simulator on PSVR 2 and PCVR.

items.title: Upcoming VR Titles Announced: Into The Radius 2 and Others
items.summary: Upcoming VR games include A Long Survive in May on PSVR 2 and PCVR, Paranormal Activity: The Lost Soul on Quest and PCVR, and Into The Radius 2 on Quest and PCVR.

items.title: Nintendo Switch 2 News: Indiana Jones and the Great Circle Footage and Release Date
items.summary: New footage and hands-on previews for Indiana Jones and the Great Circle, set for release on May 12th for Nintendo Switch 2. Rumors also swirl around Tomodachi Life and other titles like Borderlands 4 and Hogwarts Legacy.

items.title: Battlefield 6 Season 3 Update Details Revealed
items.summary: Battlefield 6 Season 3 brings major updates including mortar improvements, vehicle changes, ping system enhancements, matchmaking updates, menu overhauls, REDSEC gameplay, and new modes like ranked battle royale quads.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Canonical and Ubuntu servers offline after sustained cross-border attack
items.summary: Servers operated by Ubuntu and its parent company Canonical have been down for over a day following a sustained, cross-border cyber attack that began on Thursday.

items.title: UK court orders Samsung to pay ZTE $392M for patents
items.summary: A UK court has ordered Samsung to pay ZTE $392 million for patents essential for phone network access, with Samsung facing similar lawsuits in China, Germany, and Brazil.

items.title: Nissan abandons plans for US EV plant
items.summary: Nissan has scrapped its plans to build a US electric vehicle plant that was intended to produce 200,000 EVs annually.

items.title: Microsoft's Xbox mode rolling out to Windows 11 PCs
items.summary: Microsoft is bringing its console-like Xbox mode interface to Windows 11 PCs, enhancing gaming experience on personal computers.

items.title: Oura adds detailed hormonal health insights to Series 3 and 4 rings
items.summary: Oura is introducing more comprehensive hormonal health tracking to its Series 3 and 4 smart rings, including potential side effects of birth control methods.

items.title: Meta acquires Assured Robot Intelligence for robotics development
items.summary: Meta has acquired Assured Robot Intelligence, a startup building AI models for robots, with the team joining Meta Superintelligence Labs.

items.title: TSMC completes full exit from UK chip designer Arm with $231M share sale
items.summary: TSMC has sold its remaining shares in Arm for $231 million, marking a complete exit from the UK-based chip designer.

items.title: Sentinel-1D satellite becomes fully operational for Europe's radar mission
items.summary: The Copernicus Sentinel-1D satellite, launched last November, has completed its in-orbit commissioning and is now fully operational, completing Europe's four-satellite Sentinel-1 constellation.
